rationale: Contract-stability lens: all three claims verified. (1) ApproveSpec inherits with ZERO internal/approve change: the commit touches only 3 files (architecture.md, spec.go, spec_test.go); ApproveSpec (spec.go:47-48) calls ValidateSpec and hard-fails on vr.HasFailures(). All three new codes go through AddError -> SevError, which HasFailures() (validate.go:45) treats as a failure, so `mindspec spec approve` inherits both checks with no approve edit. `go test ./internal/approve` green. (2) Issue-code/severity surface is stable: impacted-domains-load/-resolve are REUSED verbatim from plan.go:126-127/142-144 (byte-identical load-error guard incl. errors.Unwrap+os.IsNotExist), same SevError; adr-touchpoint-missing is a NEW distinct code (deliberately separate from adr-cite-missing per spec-097 R2), adr-* convention, SevError, actionable hint. (3) No shared-helper regression: the commit modifies NO helper file (plan.go/divergence.go/domains.go/adr.go untouched); helpers are only CALLED; full `go test ./internal/validate` (incl. pre-existing plan-approve impacted-domains-resolve/adr-cite tests) green in 78-101s. SHA-blob grep confirms the two new checks emit ONLY the three intended codes; adr-coverage/adr-cite-irrelevant appear only in comments, never emitted (R7c holds).
findings: internal/approve untouched (git show --name-only 62cd6df3 = 3 files); ApproveSpec spec.go:47-48 hard-fails on vr.HasFailures(); all 3 new codes are SevError so inheritance is real — go test ./internal/approve green.

impacted-domains-load / impacted-domains-resolve reused verbatim from plan.go (same code, same SevError, byte-identical `!os.IsNotExist(errors.Unwrap(impErr))` load guard). checkImpactedDomainsResolutionParity correctly discards the normalized set with `_` (spec-approve runs no downstream consumer, R7c) where plan.go assigns `impacted = normalized` — intended divergence, not a defect.
adr-touchpoint-missing is a new, distinct code (not colliding with adr-cite-missing) with SevError and an inline recovery hint — good issue-code hygiene, follows the adr-* convention.

No shared-helper regression: commit modifies no plan.go/divergence.go/domains.go/adr.go; the helpers (loadImpactedDomains, normalizeImpactedDomains, adrStoreForSpecFn, newMemoStore, store.Get) are only invoked. Full internal/validate suite green incl. the pre-existing plan-approve tests. New package var adrTouchpointLinkRe has a unique name (no shadowing); build clean.

SHA-pinned blob grep: the two new checks emit only impacted-domains-load(222)/-resolve(226)/adr-touchpoint-missing(273); adr-coverage/adr-cite-irrelevant occur only in comment text (250) — never in an AddError/AddWarning call, so the existence-only R7c boundary is honored.

Observation (non-blocking): reusing the same impacted-domains-resolve code at both spec-approve and plan-approve means a code-based issue consumer cannot distinguish which gate raised it; this is the intended parity semantics (same meaning either gate) and matches divergence.go already sharing plan.go's code — no change requested.
